what: feature_list.json (/feature_list.json, Phase 0): canonical version-controlled coverage-model file; append-only items[] each with id/type{functional,NFR,WIRING}/priority/dependencies/acceptance_criteria/status{unproven,proven,failed default unproven}/in_scope(default true)/optional provenance/ears_pattern/evidence{test_file,test_name,output_hash,collected_at} when proven. Gates compute COMPLETE from it. Append-only; only status edges mutate. Validated by schema/feature_list.schema.json; mirrored to Postgres; Postgres-down fallback. requirements.md:114-129; design.md:286-414.

open_gaps.gap: JSON Schema does NOT enforce evidence-required-when-status-proven. CoverageItem.required omits evidence; no if/then/allOf; the constraint is only a prose description (design.md:382-383). A schema-valid file can have status=proven with no evidence. Property 2 and REQ-COV-003/006 imply file-level schema enforcement, but it lives only in PreToolUse status-guard + SubagentStop + evidence_collector; the Phase-0 schema test (design.md:623) only rejects a missing output_hash inside an existing evidence object.
open_gaps.fix: Add allOf {if status const proven then required [evidence]} to CoverageItem in schema/feature_list.schema.json and design.md:322-385; note in Req 5.3/5.6 and Property 2 the gate is jointly schema+hook enforced.

open_gaps.gap: status is in CoverageItem.required AND given default unproven (design.md:324,373); a required property's default never applies, so Property 1/Req 5.1 'status defaults to unproven' is not realizable via the schema. Same for in_scope.
open_gaps.fix: Drop status/in_scope from required OR remove the defaults and have the Initializer write them; reword Property 1/Req 5.1 to 'written with status=unproven'.

open_gaps.gap: Append-only 'never deleted or reordered' (design.md:126,288) is asserted but no schema rule or test verifies ordering/length preservation across writes; Property 3 blocks identity mutation only, so a reorder/drop keeping ids/types intact is not provably caught.
open_gaps.fix: Add a PreToolUse check + property test diffing the incoming items[] id-sequence vs the prior committed version, blocking on deletion/reorder; add to task 6.1.

open_gaps.gap: The Z3 harness has ZERO references to feature_list.json/CoverageItem/status; coverage is modeled by standalone Booleans (unproven/passed/evidence*). The three-state {unproven,proven,failed} machine and the failed edges (Req 5.8/Property 3) are NOT in Z3, so the 'machine-checked' claim over the status-transition graph is overstated.
open_gaps.fix: Add a Z3 check group encoding the three-state enum and permitted edges with all other edges UNSAT; reference from requirements.md:123/129; until then soften 'machine-checked'.

open_gaps.gap: Amendment re-entry (REQ-COV-007/Property 25/CHECK-10) targets only Postgres coverage_items.status (design.md:1152), not the feature_list.json item, which is the Phase-0 source of truth and Postgres-down fallback; on a Postgres-down amendment the file could stay proven while the DB shows unproven.
open_gaps.fix: Extend REQ-COV-007/Req 22/task 48 to also flip the feature_list.json item to unproven and re-block its feature_list_sha; add a test in task 39.15.

open_gaps.gap: Structural-invariant test coverage is thin vs the file's 'canonical/never deleted' billing: no test for dependencies[] referential integrity, priority uniqueness, provenance on inferred items (Req 2.3 requires provenance but the schema makes it OPTIONAL, design.md:365-369), or checklist_ref.sha matching a real git blob (Property 18 asserts it; no CI computes it).
open_gaps.fix: Add tests for dependencies[] closure, provenance-required-when-discovery-ran, and a CI git-blob-SHA check for checklist_ref.

open_gaps.gap: Glossary/schema mismatch: glossary (requirements.md:28) lists '...acceptance criteria, and evidence' as if evidence is always present, while the schema makes evidence optional/present only on proven items.
open_gaps.fix: Reword requirements.md:28 to '...acceptance criteria, in_scope, and (when proven) an Evidence_Record'.

open_gaps.gap: ears_pattern is in the schema enum but not in CoverageItem.required; provenance is optional — yet Req 1.4/Property 16 require exactly one EARS pattern and Req 2.3 requires provenance on inferred requirements, so both are unenforced at the file level.
open_gaps.fix: Add ears_pattern (and provenance conditionally) to CoverageItem.required, OR state in Req 1.4/2.3 that the guarantee is enforced by spec_validator.py at authoring time.

critic_findings.detail: NFR SUBTYPE FIELD IS UNREPRESENTABLE IN THE COVERAGE ITEM. Req 25.1/25.2 (REQ-VERIFY-007/008) say 'WHEN an NFR item of subtype `performance` is verified...' and '...subtype `accessibility`...' — i.e. the Verifier routes to k6/Lighthouse vs axe-core based on an NFR subtype carried on the coverage item. But the CoverageItem schema (design.md:331-385) has only `type ∈ {functional,NFR,WIRING}` and NO `subtype`/`nfr_subtype` field, and `additionalProperties` is not constrained to add one. There is no machine-readable way for feature_list.json to distinguish a performance NFR from an accessibility NFR, so the perf_a11y_verifier cannot deterministically select its evidence tool from the coverage mo…
critic_findings.cit: requirements.md:447-448 (Req 25.1/25.2); design.md:331-385 (CoverageItem schema, no subtype field)

critic_findings.detail: WIRING-TYPE EVIDENCE OBLIGATION (Req 8.3) IS UNENFORCED AT THE FILE LEVEL AND ABSENT FROM ears_stories. Req 8.3: 'WHEN a WIRING item is claimed `proven`, THE System SHALL require an integration test exercising the real execution path as the attached Evidence_Record.' The schema's `type` description even says 'WIRING items require integration-test evidence' (design.md:334), but the EvidenceRecord schema is a single uniform 4-field shape with no marker that the evidence is an *integration* test on the real path — nothing distinguishes a unit-test Evidence_Record from the integration-test the requirement demands. Task 47 tracks it generically but no schema/Property ties the WIRING type to an in…

PROPERTY 24 EVIDENCE-FIELD NAME COLLISION (`acting_agent` vs `actor_agent`) DIRECTLY ON THE COVERAGE-MODEL EVIDENCE CHAIN. design.md:473 declares 'Canonical name is actor_agent everywhere (no acting_agent)' and Property 24 (design.md:1068) uses `actor_agent`, but the Property 24 PBT (tasks.md:655, task 39.13) still asserts on `acting_agent` ('Generate evidence chains with arbitrary `acting_agent` identities; assert any Evidence_Record where `acting_agent == "implementer.md"`...'). The test that enforces Implementer-cannot-self-verify on feature_list.json's evidence checks a field name the design says does not exist, so the guarantee is mis-wired. The draft lists Property 24 among properties_…

EARS_PATTERN ENUM OMITS 'complex' WHILE THE GLOSSARY LISTS SIX PATTERNS — the draft notes ears_pattern is not in CoverageItem.required but never flags that the enum itself only has FIVE values {ubiquitous,event-driven,state-driven,unwanted,optional} (design.md:347) whereas the EARS glossary names SIX including 'Complex' (requirements.md:31). design.md:1188 resolves this in prose ('Complex denotes a documented composition of the five base patterns; no schema change'), but that resolution is not referenced from the schema, the glossary, Property 16, or Req 1.4 — so a reader hitting Req 1.4 'assign exactly one of the five EARS patterns' vs the glossary's six has an unreconciled mismatch on a fi…
critic_findings.cit: requirements.md:31 vs design.md:347,1188; Req 1.4 requirements.md:65; Property 16 design.md:1002-1006

critic_findings.detail: POSTGRES coverage_items DOES NOT MIRROR `type` OR `acceptance_criteria`, undercutting the draft's 'mirrored to Postgres / Postgres-down fallback' framing and the conservative-divergence claim. The coverage_items table (design.md:443-451) carries only id, requirement_id, status, in_scope, slice_id, updated_at — `type`, `priority`, `dependencies`, `acceptance_criteria`, `ears_pattern`, `provenance`, and `evidence` live in feature_list.json and (partially) the `requirements`/`evidence_records` tables, NOT coverage_items. The draft's behavior 'Postgres-down fallback truth' and gap-5 ('Postgres-only amendment') both assume Postgres holds the same item shape; in fact the file is the richer record …

`dependencies[]` IS NEITHER SCHEMA-VALIDATED FOR REFERENTIAL INTEGRITY NOR WIRED TO THE SCOPE-SEQUENCING GATE — a coverage-model semantics gap the draft only half-raises. The schema says dependencies are 'IDs of coverage items that must be proven before this one' (design.md:357), and Req 7.5/Property 7 block a new slice while any 'prior-slice' item is unproven — but 'prior-slice' is implemented purely by priority ordering / scope sequencing (design.md:211, Property 7), with NO check that an item's declared `dependencies[]` are proven before it is scheduled or proven. So the dependency graph encoded in feature_list.json is inert: an item can be proven while its declared dependencies are still…
critic_findings.cit: design.md:357 (dependencies desc) vs Req 7.5 requirements.md:160 / Property 7 design.md:930-934

critic_findings.detail: EMPTY items[] IS SCHEMA-VALID AND THE INITIALIZER WRITES `items: []`, but UNMAPPED/discovery completeness (Req 2.2, Property 13) and Stop/OPA gates are never reconciled with the empty-file case. tools/feature_list_init.py (tasks.md:239) produces a minimal valid file with `items: []` and no `minItems` on the items array (design.md:315-319). A feature_list.json with zero items trivially satisfies the Stop gate (no unproven in-scope items) and OPA zero-evidence policy (zero approved requirements → zero rows expected), so an all-empty coverage model would read as COMPLETE. Nothing in the draft or specs states that the gates additionally require items to be non-empty / discovery to have run befor…
critic_findings.cit: design.md:315-319 (items array, no minItems); tasks.md:239 (items: []); Req 2.2 requirements.md:78

critic_findings.detail: DRAFT CITES `design.md:766` FOR 'feature_list_sha re-blocks Writes' / 'SHA-bound at plan approval' but understates that the SHA-BINDING FORMAT IS UNDER-SPECIFIED AND ENFORCED ONLY OVER RAW FILE BYTES, not the canonical item set. The plan-approval gate compares `approval["feature_list_sha"]` to `f"sha256:{compute_sha256(feature_list.json bytes)}"` (design.md:791-792) — a hash of the literal file bytes. That means any whitespace/key-reordering/formatting change to feature_list.json (even one that preserves the canonical item set) re-blocks all writes and forces a fresh human approval, while the append-only status-flip mutations the design EXPECTS (unproven→proven) ALSO change the bytes and thu…
critic_findings.cit: design.md:766,789-801 vs design.md:288 (status flip is the expected mutation)

critic_findings.detail: DRAFT'S gap-2 (status/in_scope in `required` defeats the schema `default`) IS CORRECT but UNDERSTATES THE BLAST RADIUS: the SAME required-vs-default defect also makes the Postgres mirror authoritative-by-default divergent. coverage_items.status has `DEFAULT 'unproven'` and in_scope `DEFAULT TRUE` at the DB layer (design.md:446-448), so a row inserted without those columns DOES default — but the JSON schema's defaults never fire (because required). Thus 'defaults to unproven' is realized in Postgres but NOT in the file, so a hand/tool-written feature_list.json that omits status is schema-INVALID while the equivalent DB row is valid-and-defaulted. The draft frames this as a single schema incon…
critic_findings.cit: design.md:324,373,378 (required+default in JSON schema) vs design.md:446-448 (DB DEFAULTs)

ears_needing_work.criterion: in_scope default true; gates count only in_scope

citation_issues: requirements.md:124 is cited for both Req 5.3/REQ-COV-003 AND Req 5.6/REQ-COV-006 in the ears_stories entry. Line 124 is Req 5 criterion 3 (REQ-COV-003: four-field Evidence_Record required on proven transition). Req 5 criterion 6 (REQ-COV-006: same four-field evidence definition, rejection if any absent) is at line 127, not line 124. The citation should be requirements.md:124,127 to cover both sub-requirements.
